seoTitle: How to Create a New Trello Workspace
metaDescription: Set up a new Trello Workspace to organize department boards, invite collaborators, and manage visibility and branding from scratch.
intro: A dedicated Workspace container keeps your boards, templates, and department workflows from turning into a chaotic pile of unrelated projects. This guide covers building a new Workspace from scratch, from naming it to inviting your first collaborators.

steps.heading: Find the creation panel
steps.body: From your dashboard header, locate the master creation matrix for building a new workspace.

steps.heading: Name and categorize your workspace
steps.body: Assign a precise tracking name and choose an appropriate category or industry template.

steps.heading: Write a workspace description
steps.body: Add relevant context in the description field so members understand its purpose at a glance.

steps.heading: Invite initial members
steps.body: Generate a shareable onboarding link or enter team email addresses to add collaborators.

steps.heading: Skip invites if you're building privately
steps.body: Use an alternative layout command to bypass early member steps and polish your space before opening it up.

steps.heading: Add branding
steps.body: Upload a custom logo to help distributed teams quickly identify your project dashboard.

faq.question: Do I have to invite people right when I create a workspace?
faq.answer: No, you can bypass the invite step and build out your workspace privately before adding collaborators.

faq.question: Can I categorize my workspace by industry?
faq.answer: Yes, you can assign a functional workspace industry field when setting it up.

faq.question: How do I invite team members later?
faq.answer: You can generate a shareable onboarding link or enter email addresses whenever you're ready to add collaborators.

faq.question: Can I customize how my workspace looks to others?
faq.answer: Yes, adding a custom branding logo helps distributed teams quickly recognize the workspace.
